questions.question: Explain the host-device programming model in CUDA with the typical execution flow of a heterogeneous program.
keyPoints: Host is the CPU with system memory; device is the GPU with device memory
Typical flow: allocate GPU memory, copy data H2D, launch kernel, copy results D2H, free memory
nvcc compiler separates host code (system compiler) from device code (GPU compiler)

questions.question: Discuss the PCIe bandwidth bottleneck in CPU-GPU heterogeneous systems. Describe strategies to minimize data transfer overhead, including pinned memory, CUDA streams, and Unified Memory.
keyPoints: PCIe bandwidth (16-64 GB/s) is much lower than GPU memory bandwidth (2+ TB/s)
Pinned memory enables DMA transfers and higher bandwidth, required for async copies
CUDA streams allow overlapping data transfer with kernel execution (pipelining)
Unified Memory simplifies programming with automatic page migration but has page fault overhead
Minimize transfers by keeping data on GPU, batching transfers, and maximizing compute-to-transfer ratio
